F-001.evidence: Recommended Direction now presents Option A (validateWorktreePath) as preferred, with concrete before/after code examples showing the replacement of isPathSafe() with validateWorktreePath(customTargetPath, this.config.basePath!).

F-002.evidence: Impact section now includes 'Directory creation outside CM_ROOT_DIR' bullet detailing executeClone() mkdirSync(parentDir, { recursive: true }) risk at clone-manager.ts:382-385. Affected Code section lists clone-manager.ts:382-385 explicitly.

F-003.evidence: Impact section now includes 'DB persistence of invalid paths' bullet covering onCloneSuccess() at clone-manager.ts:479-515, with explanation of secondary security risk from createRepository() and scanWorktrees(). Affected Code lists clone-manager.ts:479-515.

F-004.evidence: Root Cause section now includes 'Additional input sanitization gap (route.ts:96)' paragraph. Recommended Direction includes 'Additional fix: route.ts input sanitization' subsection with concrete code example (targetDir?.trim() || undefined). Affected Code lists route.ts L49 and L96.

F-005.evidence: New 'Acceptance Criteria (Test Requirements)' section added with 8 numbered test cases covering: relative path resolution, consistent path usage, DB absolute path storage, mkdirSync containment, path traversal rejection, no information leakage, existing absolute path backward compatibility, and existsSync resolved path verification. Additionally, 4 detailed test scenarios (A-D) are provided with concrete path examples.

F-006.evidence: Severity section now includes 'Attack preconditions' with two bullet points covering CM_AUTH_TOKEN_HASH set (insider threat) vs unset (full external attack surface) scenarios.

F-007.evidence: Root Cause Step 1 now contains a parenthetical note: 'Safe path: when customTargetPath is absent, getTargetPath(repoName) returns path.join(this.config.basePath!, repoName) -- an absolute path under basePath. The vulnerability is confined to the customTargetPath branch.' Summary also has a corresponding Note paragraph.

F-008.evidence: Option A code example includes a comment block: 'IMPORTANT (D4-001): Do NOT expose the exception message to the client...' with explanation of rootDir leakage risk. A dedicated 'Security note (D4-001)' paragraph follows the code block.

IF-001.evidence: Acceptance Criteria expanded from 6 to 8 items. New section 'Detailed test scenarios for relative path (IF-001)' added with 4 concrete scenarios (A-D) providing specific path inputs and expected outputs. Criteria #7 (existing absolute path backward compat) and #8 (existsSync resolved path) added.

IF-002.evidence: New 'Error handling note (IF-002)' paragraph added to Option A explaining the exception-vs-boolean pattern mismatch and providing two approaches: minimal-scope try-catch, or a helper function (resolveCustomTargetPath) returning string|null.

IF-003.evidence: Review History notes IF-003 as 'skipped: URL double-decoding design in path-validator.ts noted as future refactoring candidate, out of scope for this Issue.' This is an appropriate disposition for a nice_to_have item outside the Issue's scope.

IF-004.evidence: New 'PR Checklist' section added with explicit item: 'Existing test (clone-manager.test.ts, absolute path customPath test) passes without modification (IF-004 confirmation)'. Acceptance Criteria #7 also addresses this.

IF-005.evidence: Impact section now includes 'No impact on job status/cancel' bullet explaining getCloneJobStatus() and cancelCloneJob() do not reference targetPath. Affected Code lists L594-644 with 'confirmed NO impact' annotation.

IF-006.evidence: Review History notes IF-006 as 'skipped: Frontend api-client.ts does not send targetDir -- noted as informational, no Issue changes needed.' Appropriate disposition.

IF-007.evidence: Review History notes IF-007 as 'skipped: DB schema confirmed unaffected -- no Issue changes needed.' Appropriate disposition.

IF-008.evidence: Review History notes IF-008 as 'skipped: opencode-config.ts local validateWorktreePath() name collision -- informational, no Issue changes needed.' Appropriate disposition.

summary: All 8 findings from Stage 1 (F-001 through F-008) and all 8 findings from Stage 3 (IF-001 through IF-008) have been appropriately addressed in the updated Issue. The Issue is now comprehensive, technically accurate, and implementation-ready. The Review History section provides full traceability of all changes. One minor new observation (N-001) and one nice-to-have suggestion (N-002) were identified, neither of which warrants blocking implementation.

findings.title: Acceptance Criteria #8 description refers to existsSync but should also mention mkdirSync
findings.description: Acceptance Criteria #8 states: 'existsSync uses resolved path: Verify via vi.mocked(existsSync) call argument inspection that the resolved absolute path (not the original relative string) is passed to existsSync()'. This is correct but is a subset of Criteria #2 (consistent path usage). Criteria #2 already requires 'the resolved absolute path is the same value used in existsSync(), job persistence, mkdirSync(), git clone spawn, and DB registration.' Criteria #8 could be considered redundant with #2, but having an explicit existsSync verification criterion is helpful for test specificity, so this is not a problem -- just an observation.
findings.recommendation: No change needed. The redundancy between #2 and #8 is acceptable because #8 specifies the concrete verification mechanism (vi.mocked inspection) while #2 states the general requirement.

findings.title: Validation Notes item #4 (trim whitespace) lacks a concrete test scenario in Acceptance Criteria
findings.description: Validation Notes include: '4. targetDir with leading/trailing whitespace is properly trimmed'. However, the Acceptance Criteria (Test Requirements) section does not have a specific test case for the route.ts trim() fix. The 8 numbered criteria and 4 detailed scenarios (A-D) all focus on clone-manager.ts behavior. A trim() test would belong in a route.ts integration test or API-level test rather than in clone-manager.test.ts, which may explain its omission. This is a minor gap since the trim() fix in route.ts is straightforward (single line change) and the PR Checklist already requires `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit` to pass.
findings.location: Issue body, Validation Notes #4 vs Acceptance Criteria section
findings.recommendation: Optionally add a note that the route.ts trim() behavior should be verified in an integration or API-level test, or add a 9th acceptance criterion specifically for it. Alternatively, this can be left as-is since the fix is trivial and the Validation Notes section serves as a reminder.

overall_assessment: Issue #392 has been comprehensively updated across two review iterations and is now in excellent shape for implementation. All 16 findings from the first iteration (5 should_fix + 3 nice_to_have from Stage 1, plus 1 must_fix + 4 should_fix + 3 nice_to_have from Stage 3) have been addressed in the Issue body. The key improvements include: (1) Option A with validateWorktreePath() as the preferred approach with D4-001 security note, (2) full impact analysis covering mkdirSync, DB persistence, and job status/cancel methods, (3) 8 concrete Acceptance Criteria with 4 detailed test scenarios, (4) PR Checklist with backward compatibility confirmation, (5) route.ts input sanitization guidance, and (6) error handling pattern guidance for exception-to-CloneResult conversion. The Issue's technical claims align with the actual codebase (verified against clone-manager.ts, path-validator.ts, route.ts, and clone-manager.test.ts). The Review History section provides full audit trail. No must_fix or should_fix items remain. The Issue is ready for implementation.

code_verification.clone-manager.ts:337: Confirmed: targetPath = customTargetPath || this.getTargetPath(repoName) -- unresolved customTargetPath used directly
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:341: Confirmed: isPathSafe(customTargetPath, this.config.basePath!) validates but does not resolve
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:382-385: Confirmed: mkdirSync on parentDir of unresolved targetPath
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:389: Confirmed: spawn('git', ['clone', ..., targetPath]) uses unresolved path
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:479-515: Confirmed: onCloneSuccess() persists unresolved targetPath to DB
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:594-614: Confirmed: getCloneJobStatus() does NOT reference targetPath
code_verification.clone-manager.ts:619-644: Confirmed: cancelCloneJob() does NOT reference targetPath
code_verification.path-validator.ts:29-68: Confirmed: isPathSafe() returns boolean only, does not return resolved path
code_verification.path-validator.ts:89-117: Confirmed: validateWorktreePath() returns resolved absolute path, throws on invalid
code_verification.path-validator.ts:102-104: Confirmed: Error message contains rootDir value (leakage risk noted in D4-001)
code_verification.route.ts:96: Confirmed: targetDir passed without trim() to startCloneJob()
code_verification.clone-manager.test.ts:208-225: Confirmed: Only absolute path customPath test exists, no relative path test
